name: csv-dedup
about: Merge, deduplicate, and combine CSV files

outer.about: Full outer join — all rows from all files, deduped on keys

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup outer a.csv b.csv -k id -c id,name,email -o merged.csv

inner.about: Inner join — only rows whose keys appear in all input files

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup inner a.csv b.csv c.csv -k id -o common.csv

left.about: Left join — source rows only, optionally enriched from other files

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup left main.csv enrichment.csv -k domain,name -o enriched.csv

exclude.about: Keep only source rows whose keys don't appear in any other file

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup exclude main.csv dupes1.csv dupes2.csv -k domain,name

unique.about: Keep only rows whose keys appear in exactly one file

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup unique list1.csv list2.csv list3.csv -k id -o only-in-one.csv

overlap.about: Keep only rows whose keys appear in more than one file

options.about: Comma-separated key column(s) for matching rows (required)
Comma-separated columns to include in output (default: all)
Output file path (default: <first-file>.out.csv)
Show this help

examples: csv-dedup overlap list1.csv list2.csv -k domain,name -o overlaps.csv
